name: Julian Bream
abstract: Julian Alexander Bream  (15 July 1933 – 14 August 2020) was an English classical guitarist and lutenist. Regarded as one of the most distinguished classical guitarists of the 20th century, he played a significant role in improving the public perception of the classical guitar as a respectable instrument.

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: I fell in love with the music of — aside from jazz players — the music of Julian Bream, a great English guitarist and I heard his interpretation of the Ravel “Pavanne”
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: I got hold of the record of Julian Bream playing the Bach lute suites on classical guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016 [interview 1 of 2]
